title: Backspace String Compare
description:
Backspace String Compare

Given two strings s and t, return true if they are equal when both are typed into empty text editors. '#' means a backspace character.

Note that after backspacing an empty text, the text will continue empty.

Examples:

  • Input: s = "ab#c", t = "ad#c"
    Output: true
    Explanation: Both s and t become "ac".
  • Input: s = "ab##", t = "c#d#"
    Output: true
    Explanation: Both s and t become "".
  • Input: s = "a#c", t = "b"
    Output: false
    Explanation: s becomes "c" while t becomes "b".

Constraints:

  • 1 <= s.length, t.length <= 200
  • s and t only contain lowercase letters and '#' characters.
solution_text:
Backspace String Compare

Algorithm:

  1. Build the final string for both s and t using a stack
  2. For each character, if it's '#', pop from stack (if non-empty)
  3. Otherwise, push the character onto the stack
  4. Compare the two resulting stacks

Time Complexity: O(n + m) where n and m are the lengths of s and t

Space Complexity: O(n + m) for the two stacks

Key Insights:

  • Stack naturally handles the backspace operation (push characters, pop on '#')
  • Process both strings independently, then compare results
  • Handle edge case where '#' appears when stack is empty
